# defectdojo_jira_product_configuration (Data Source)

Data source for a DefectDojo Jira Product Configuration

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "defectdojo_jira_product_configuration" "example" {
  id = "1"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `id` (String) Identifier

### Read-Only

- `add_vulnerability_id_to_jira_label` (Boolean) Whether to add the vulnerability ID to the Jira label.
- `component` (String) The Jira component to use for issues.
- `default_assignee` (String) JIRA default assignee (name).
- `enable_engagement_epic_mapping` (Boolean) Whether to map engagements to epics in Jira
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the Jira integration is enabled.
- `engagement_id` (String) The ID of the Engagement
- `epic_issue_type_name` (String) The name of the structure that represents an Epic.
- `issue_template_dir` (String) The folder containing Django templates used to render the JIRA issue description.
- `jira_instance_id` (String) The ID of the Jira Instance
- `jira_labels` (String) JIRA issue labels space separated.
- `product_id` (String) The ID of the Product
- `product_jira_sla_notification` (Boolean) Send SLA notifications as comments
- `project_key` (String) The Jira Project Key
- `push_all_issues` (Boolean) Automatically maintain parity with JIRA.
- `push_notes` (Boolean) Whether to push notes to Jira
- `risk_acceptance_expiration_notification` (Boolean) Send Risk Acceptance expiration notifications as comments
